Stripe test mode vs live mode: common mistakes during launch
body:
Switching from Stripe test mode to live mode is error-prone. These are the most common issues developers hit when going live.

**Mistake 1 — Wrong API keys:**
```typescript
// .env.local (development)
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_xxx      // Test key
STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_xxx

// .env.production (production) — MUST use live keys
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_xxx      // Live key
STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_live_xxx
```

**Mistake 2 — Webhook endpoints not configured for live mode:**
Stripe test and live modes have separate webhook endpoints. You must add your production webhook URL in live mode too:
```bash
# Test mode webhook (Stripe CLI)
stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/webhooks/stripe

# Live mode — configure in Stripe Dashboard
# Dashboard > Webhooks > Add endpoint > https://myapp.com/api/webhooks/stripe
# Select events: checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.*
```

**Mistake 3 — Test price IDs in production code:**
```typescript
// BAD: Hardcoded test price IDs
const PRICE_ID = 'price_1234test';  // This won't work in live mode!

// GOOD: Use environment variables
const PRICE_ID = process.env.STRIPE_PRO_PRICE_ID!;
```

**Mistake 4 — Forgetting to verify webhook secret per environment:**
```
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_test_xxx  // Test
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_live_xxx  // Live — DIFFERENT secret!
```

**Launch checklist:**
- Switch API keys to `sk_live_*` / `pk_live_*`
- Create products/prices in live mode (they don't carry over from test)
- Configure live webhook endpoint with correct events
- Update webhook signing secret
- Test with a real card ($0.50 charge, then refund)
- Enable Stripe Radar for fraud protection
